entries.label: Basic math
entries.code:
let sum = 5 + 3;      // 8
let diff = 10 - 4;    // 6
let product = 3 * 7;  // 21
let quotient = 10 / 3; // 3 (integer division)
let remainder = 10 % 3; // 1
entries.note: Standard arithmetic; integer division truncates

entries.label: Float arithmetic
entries.note: Float division gives decimal results

entries.label: Compound assignment
entries.note: Modify in place with op= operators

entries.label: Overflow handling
entries.note: Four strategies: wrapping, saturating, overflowing, checked

entries.label: Operator traits
entries.note: Implement operator traits from std::ops to overload operators

entries.label: Equality
entries.code:
5 == 5   // true
5 != 3   // true
"hello" == "hello"  // true
entries.note: == and != require PartialEq trait

entries.label: Ordering
entries.code:
3 < 5    // true
5 > 3    // true
3 <= 3   // true
5 >= 6   // false
entries.note: Comparison operators require PartialOrd trait

entries.label: Chained comparisons
entries.note: No chaining; use logical AND to combine comparisons

entries.label: Custom comparison
entries.note: Derive or manually implement PartialOrd for custom ordering

entries.label: Boolean logic
entries.note: && and || are short-circuit: right side not evaluated if left determines result

entries.label: Short-circuit examples
entries.note: Short-circuiting prevents panics from invalid access

entries.label: Bitwise operations
entries.note: Work on integer types; useful for flags and bit manipulation

entries.label: Shift operations
entries.note: Left shift multiplies by power of 2; right shift divides

entries.label: Compound bitwise
entries.code:
let mut flags: u8 = 0;
flags |= 0b0001;  // set bit 0
flags |= 0b0100;  // set bit 2
flags &= !0b0001; // clear bit 0
let has_bit2 = flags & 0b0100 != 0; // check bit 2
entries.note: Common pattern for bit flags: set with |=, clear with &= !, test with &

entries.label: Reference
entries.note: & borrows immutably; &mut borrows mutably

entries.label: Dereference
entries.note: * dereferences a reference to access the inner value

entries.label: Auto-deref
entries.note: Rust auto-dereferences for method calls; no need for explicit *

entries.label: Deref coercion
entries.note: Rust automatically applies Deref chain: &String -> &str, &Vec<T> -> &[T]

entries.label: Range types
entries.code:
let r = 0..5;     // Range: 0, 1, 2, 3, 4
let r = 0..=5;    // RangeInclusive: 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5
let r = ..5;      // RangeTo: ..5
let r = 5..;      // RangeFrom: 5, 6, 7, ...
let r = ..;       // RangeFull
entries.note: Used for slicing, iteration, and pattern matching

entries.label: In for loops
entries.note: Ranges implement Iterator; work with chars too

entries.label: In slicing
entries.note: Ranges used as indices for slicing arrays, Vecs, and strings

entries.label: contains
entries.note: Check if a value is within a range

entries.label: Error propagation
entries.note: ? unwraps Ok/Some or returns early with Err/None

entries.label: With Option
entries.note: ? on Option returns None if None; unwraps Some

entries.label: Chaining
entries.note: Chain multiple ? operators for clean error propagation

entries.label: Numeric casting
entries.note: as for primitive type conversions; may truncate or wrap

entries.label: Pointer casting
entries.note: Cast references to raw pointers and to usize addresses

entries.label: Casting gotchas
entries.note: as casts can silently lose data; use TryFrom for safe conversion
